description_long: This object is a small, stubby candle with an irregular, organic shape, suggesting it has been partially melted and used. The surface is uneven, with visible wax drips and a rough, textured appearance, especially around the base where the wax has pooled and solidified. The top of the candle is slightly indented, likely where the wick would be, though the wick itself is not clearly visible. The color is a pale, off-white or light yellow, typical of natural or unscented wax. There is no visible flame, and the candle does not appear to be housed in any holder or container. Its compact size and rustic look make it suitable for placement on a tabletop or as part of an outdoor urban memorial or decorative arrangement.
description: A small, pale, partially melted candle with visible wax drips and a rough, irregular surface. It is freestanding and lacks a holder.
description_view_0: The front view shows the candle's uneven, melted surface and pooled wax at the base.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the irregular, organic shape and the rough texture of the wax drips.
description_view_2: The rear view displays more of the pooled wax and the candle's stubby, squat form.
description_view_3: This side view again emphasizes the rough, melted appearance and the lack of a visible wick or holder.
